data.content:
## Implementation Plan

### Summary
Extend the scope refinement timeout from 3m to 10m, and add automatic agent-retry-on-failure using the refinement dispatch pool. When the initial agent fails/times out, select an alternative agent (excluding the failed one) and retry once before falling through to the existing re-enqueue logic.

### Complexity: Low-Medium
- 4 files modified, ~50 lines of new/changed code
- 1 new test file for retry logic
- No schema or API changes required

### Changes

#### 1. Update timeout constant (scope-refinement-prompt.ts:15)
- Change PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS from 180_000 to 600_000 (10 minutes)
- The error message already uses PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS / 1000 dynamically — no message change needed

#### 2. Update diagnostics stall threshold (scope-refinement-diagnostics-card.tsx:27)
- Change STALL_THRESHOLD_MS from 180_000 to 600_000 to match the new timeout
- This prevents jobs from being incorrectly flagged as "Stalled" during normal operation

#### 3. Add agent parameter to runScopeRefinementPrompt (scope-refinement-prompt.ts:267-287)
- Add optional agent?: AgentTarget parameter to runScopeRefinementPrompt()
- When provided, use the passed agent instead of calling getScopeRefinementAgent()
- This enables the worker to specify an alternative agent on retry

#### 4. Add agent-retry logic in processScopeRefinementJob (scope-refinement-worker.ts:150-253)
- When runScopeRefinementPrompt() throws (timeout or other failure):
  1. Extract the agentId from the agent that just failed
  2. Load settings and call resolvePoolAgent("scope_refinement", pools, agents, failedAgentId) to get an alternative
  3. If an alternative agent is found, retry runScopeRefinementPrompt() once with the alternative agent
  4. If no alternative exists, log "no alternative agent available in scope_refinement pool for retry" and fall through to maybeReenqueue()
  5. If the retry also fails, fall through to maybeReenqueue() with the retry error
- This retry is inline (single immediate retry), not another re-enqueue cycle. The existing maybeReenqueue() logic (MAX_RETRIES=2) still governs re-enqueue attempts.

#### 5. Test strategy
- Unit test in src/lib/__tests__/scope-refinement-worker-retry.test.ts:
  - Verify that when the first agent call fails, a retry is attempted with an alternative agent from the pool
  - Verify that when no alternative agent exists, the job fails with clear error messaging
  - Verify that when the retry also fails, the job is re-enqueued via existing logic
  - Verify that successful first attempts don't trigger retry logic
- Existing tests: Run scope-refinement-worker.test.ts to verify no regressions

### Dependencies & Risks
- No breaking changes: The agent parameter is optional — all existing call sites continue to work
- Behavioral change: Jobs that previously timed out at 3m now run up to 10m. This is intentional but means workers are occupied longer on slow jobs. With MAX_WORKERS=2, this could reduce throughput if many jobs are slow. Acceptable given the stated goal.
- Pool availability: In simple dispatch mode (non-advanced), resolvePoolAgent won't find pool entries, so the retry-with-alternative path won't activate. The timeout extension still applies. This is expected — retry requires a pool with multiple agents.

### File summary
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| src/lib/scope-refinement-prompt.ts | Timeout 180s->600s, optional agent param |
| src/lib/scope-refinement-worker.ts | Agent-retry logic in processScopeRefinementJob |
| src/components/scope-refinement-diagnostics-card.tsx | Stall threshold 180s->600s |
| src/lib/__tests__/scope-refinement-worker-retry.test.ts | New: retry fallback tests |
